text: MAML (Model-Agnostic Meta-Learning, Finn et al., ICML 2017) trains a model initialization that can adapt to any new task with just a few gradient steps — the inner loop fine-tunes on a specific task while the outer loop optimizes the initialization for fast adaptation across tasks.

Reptile (Nichol et al., 2018) simplifies MAML by using a first-order approximation — repeatedly sample a task, train on it for k steps, and move the initialization toward the final trained weights — achieving comparable performance without requiring second-order derivatives.
